steps.title: Compute the result
steps.content: Simplify: $$g(3) = \sqrt{25} = 5.$$ That matches choice C — C is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice C is correct. It's given that the function $g$ is defined by $g\left(x\right)=\sqrt{8x+1}$. Substituting $3$ for $x$ in the given function yields $g\left(3\right)=\sqrt{8\left(3\right)+1}$, which is equivalent to $g\left(3\right)=\sqrt{25}$, or $g\left(3\right)=5$. Therefore, the value of $g\left(3\right)$ is $5$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice D is incorrect. This is the value of $8\left(3\right)+1$, not $\sqrt{8\left(3\right)+1}$.
